name: La Paz (Bolivia)

colors.symbolism: the blood spilt by the patriots of the Revolution of La Paz of 16 July 1809
the fertile lands and hopes of the people of La Paz

description: Two equal horizontal stripes — ponceau red on top, emerald green on bottom.
history: The flag was created by the revolutionaries of 16 July 1809 and first hoisted on 31 July 1809 by Victorio García Lanza during the Revolution of La Paz — one of the earliest uprisings of the Spanish American wars of independence. It was carried by José Miguel Lanza throughout the war of independence. Although used continuously thereafter, it was given no formal legal status until 2 July 1987, when Prefect Ángel Gómez decreed that the official flag of the city and Department of La Paz should consist of two equal horizontal stripes of ponceau red and emerald green.

features.symbolism: red for the blood of the revolutionaries who declared independence on 16 July 1809; green for the fertile lands and the hopes of the Paceño people
